pattern: Bucketing values by an O(log v) key (here the digit sum) lets you keep only the best one or two entries per bucket and combine them in a single pass.
hints: Two numbers can only pair if they share the same digit sum, so group the numbers by that key.
Within each group you only ever want the two largest values; track the best running maximum per key.
Maintain a map from digit-sum to the largest number seen so far. When a new number matches, update the answer with their sum, then keep whichever number is larger for that key.

edge_inputs.description: no two numbers share a digit sum
single element cannot form a pair
two equal values pair with themselves
max-magnitude values both have digit sum 1
largest two numbers have different digit sums so are not the answer
several values share one digit sum; best pair is the two biggest of them

stress.description: random positive ints; many collisions on small digit sums
stress.note: frequent digit-sum collisions stress the per-key best tracking
